content.text:
## Summary Based on my exploration of the codebase, here's what I found: ### 1. **src/app/ Directory Structure** ``` src/app/ ├── (dashboard)/ │ ├── accounts/ │ │ └── page.tsx # Accounts page │ └── layout.tsx # Dashboard layout ├── page.tsx # Sign-in page (/) ├── sign-up/ │ └── page.tsx # Sign-up page ├── layout.tsx # Root layout ├── error.tsx ├── not-found.tsx └── favicon.ico ``` **Current Routes:** - `/` → Sign-in - `/sign-up` → Sign-up page - `/accounts` → Accounts page (within dashboard group) ### 2. **Settings Route Status** **⚠️ IMPORTANT:** There is **NO `/settings` route currently implemented** in the app directory. However: - The sidebar has a Settings link pointing to `/settings` (line 243 in AppSidebar.tsx) - Mobile navigation also has a Settings button pointing to `/settings` (line 85 in MobileNav.tsx) - Both i18n files (English and Russian) define `sidebar.settings` and `nav.settings` translations This means the UI is prepared for a settings route, but the actual page doesn't exist yet. ### 3. **Sidebar Navigation Component** **File:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/widgets/app-sidebar/ui/AppSidebar.tsx` Key points: - Lines 99-112 define nav items: `dashboard`, `accounts`, `members`, `partnership` (no settings here) - Line 243-246: Settings link in the user card at bottom - Settings icon has a fixed design (not affected by active state) - line 79-86 - Sidebar is used in AppLayout which wraps the dashboard content ### 4. **Dashboard Layout File** **File:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx` The layout: - Checks for authentication (accessToken, user, emailVerified, tenantId) - Wraps all dashboard children with `` component - Uses Zustand's `useSessionStore` for auth state - Returns null during hydration to prevent mismatches ### 5. **AppLayout Widget** **File:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/widgets/app-layout/ui/AppLayout.tsx` Structure: ```tsx
// Left sidebar // Mobile header
{children}
// Mobile bottom nav
``` ### 6. **Navigation Components** - **Desktop:** `AppSidebar` in `src/widgets/app-sidebar/` - **Mobile:** `MobileHeader` + `MobileBottomNav` in `src/widgets/mobile-nav/` - Both mobile and desktop have settings links pointing to `/settings` ### Key Findings: The app is **expecting a `/settings` route to exist** (both navigation components link to it), but it's not implemented yet. To complete the feature, you would need to create: - `/src/app/(dashboard)/settings/page.tsx` (to keep it within the dashboard auth guard) - OR `/src/app/settings/page.tsx` (if it should be outside the dashboard group)
